framework.name: Hardware-Backed Signing as Trust Anchor
framework.description: AP2 requires mandate signing via hardware secure enclaves (TPM, Android DPC), not software keys. This means the portable spending authority is bound to a physical device, not truly portable across agents/environments. The 'portability' is of the credential, not the signing capability.
framework.raw_quote: Signatures use hardware keys (e.g., Android DPC); verification via public key chains

source.title: Secure Use of the Agent Payments Protocol (AP2)
